title: Daily Source Digest
note: Daily source-balanced news digest — morning update.

stories.title: Trump says no nuke deal with Saudis until they sign Abraham Accords
stories.summary:
Trump says no nuke deal with Saudis until they sign Abraham Accords. Any nuclear deal between the U.S. and Saudi Arabia would be contingent on Riyadh normalizing relations with Israel and would prohibit the production of atomic weapons, President Trump said Thursday, one day after the agreement, signed Wednesday in Washington, set off alarms in both Jerusalem and Washington.

Al Jazeera: US-Saudi nuclear deal hinges on normalisation with Israel. Al Jazeera reports that president Trump said a proposed US-Saudi civilian nuclear deal is subject to Saudi Arabia joining the Abraham Accords.

stories.title: Trump slaps new tariffs on 60 trading partners, backfilling blanket tariffs Congress let expire
stories.summary:
Trump slaps new tariffs on 60 trading partners, backfilling blanket tariffs Congress let expire. The Trump administration said Thursday it is imposing tariffs of 10% to 12.5% on imports from 60 countries that failed to enforce bans on the importation of goods that use forced labor.

Al Jazeera: Trump imposes new double-digit tariffs on dozens of countries.

Al Jazeera reports that with existing 10 percent levies set to expire, Trump issues new tariffs on 60 countries under forced labour provision.

stories.title: Oil Prices Surge Back Across Dire Milestone After Houthis Enter Iran War
stories.summary:
Oil Prices Surge Back Across Dire Milestone After Houthis Enter Iran War. Oil prices climbed above $100 a barrel Thursday for the first time since May as markets reacted to a major escalation in the war with Iran.

Brent crude, the international benchmark, briefly traded above the $100 threshold after Iranian-backed Houthi militants attacked two oil tankers transiting the Bab el-Mandeb Strait in the Red Sea. The ...

stories.title: 2 dead in flash floods caused by heavy rain in West Virginia, governor's office says
stories.summary:
2 dead in flash floods caused by heavy rain in West Virginia, governor's office says. Two people have died in flash floods caused by torrential rains in West Virginia this week, the governor's office said Thursday.

Al Jazeera: Deadly monsoon floods kill at least 40 in India.

Al Jazeera reports that at least 40 people have died and up to 80 remain missing after heavy monsoon rains triggered severe flooding.

stories.summary:

“Congratulations everyone on this historic trip, this great journey that you’re on,” Trump said.

stories.title: Over 3,000 Alaska voters asked to verify citizenship before midterm elections
stories.summary:
Over 3,000 Alaska voters asked to verify citizenship before midterm elections. More than 3,000 Alaska voters have been asked to verify their U.S. citizenship ahead of next month’s primary election, an unusually large number that has drawn scrutiny from lawmakers amid a broader push by the Trump administration for states to strengthen citizenship checks before the 2026 midterm elections.

The Alaska Division of Elections said it […]. Washington Times: Alaska sends 3,000 letters asking voters to verify their citizenship ahead of this year's elections.

Washington Times reports that the state has sent more than 3,000 letters to Alaska voters asking them to verify their citizenship ahead of the Aug. 18 primary, a number a top state elections official said is unusually high.

stories.title: Matt Wright representing himself in lawsuit brought by co-star's widow
stories.summary:
Matt Wright representing himself in lawsuit brought by co-star's widow. A Federal Court case against celebrity crocodile wrangler Matt Wright could take another year before making it to trial.

The lawsuit has been brought by Danielle Wilson, the widow of Mr Wright's TV co-star Chris "Willow" Wilson, who died in a 2022 chopper crash.

stories.title: I Made This Just For You by Chris Ames review – absurd satirical takes on the bleak absurdity of modern life
stories.summary:
I Made This Just For You by Chris Ames review – absurd satirical takes on the bleak absurdity of modern life. These playful and experimental short stories are set in a distorted but plausible version of our deeply online world – but tenderness is never far Sinkholes open up all over Melbourne in one of the standout stories in Chris Ames’ funny and absurd debut collection, I Made This Just For You.

Alison, the disaffected narrator of That Is Not A Cat, finds one in her back yard, “small as a beach ball but with a devouring abyss that yawns on forever”. Late one night, she confides in it: “.

stories.title: Synthetic Sincerity review – Marc Isaacs’ AI interrogation grapples with identity and existence
stories.summary:
Synthetic Sincerity review – Marc Isaacs’ AI interrogation grapples with identity and existence. A combination of fact and fiction leaves the celebrated documentarian’s puzzling project about software training wanting for depth Marc Isaacs’ new film is a curious, intriguing, semi-sincere affair that I couldn’t make friends with.

It is an odd, shallow piece of work about artificial intelligence that is itself exasperatingly artificial, a self-aware docudrama hybrid. Isaacs is, or rather pretends to be, licensing the vivid characters from his previous, acclaimed documentaries to a fi.

stories.title: The roaring 20s: how the current decade revolutionised cinema
stories.summary:
The roaring 20s: how the current decade revolutionised cinema. Pre-Covid, the movie landscape was dramatically different: YouTubers weren’t dominating the box office, video game spin-offs rarely made an impact and Winnie-the-Pooh wasn’t yet the star of a slasher movie.

Why such a flurry of innovation – and what will be next?

Cinema has always been a medium of research and development. At any given point, the industry is searching for yet another breakthrough that can drive it into the future.

Synchronised sound in the 1920s. Technicolor in the 193.

stories.title: There Are No Ghosts at the Grand – stripping back the physical and metaphorical layers of a haunted hotel
stories.summary:
There Are No Ghosts at the Grand – stripping back the physical and metaphorical layers of a haunted hotel. Renovate rooms, investigate the supernatural and watch people burst into song in this surreal British ghost story A young American man arrives in a dilapidated British seaside town to take on the renovations of an equally run-down hotel, inherited from a mysteriously absent father he never knew.

His power tools talk to him in a Scottish accent, the local people speak in baffling slang, and occasionally people burst into song. Oh, and there are ghosts.

There Are No Ghosts at the Gr.

stories.title: Chasing new skills, going back to basics and pushing for collective action: how software engineers are adapting to AI
stories.summary:
Chasing new skills, going back to basics and pushing for collective action: how software engineers are adapting to AI. Software engineering was one of the best-paying professions in the US in 2022, but the advent of AI has disrupted it, leading to several layoffs and underemployment Every weekday, Matt, a software engineer, looks forward to his four-hour train commute to Pawling, New York.

It’s time he uses to work on his own project: a browser-based video game for which he writes every line of code himself. “I am actively trying to keep my axe sharp,” said Matt, who did not want to use his actual.

stories.title: P2M shabu seized, 2 alleged traffickers nabbed in Batangas City
stories.summary:
P2M shabu seized, 2 alleged traffickers nabbed in Batangas City. LUCENA CITY – Anti-narcotics operatives arrested two suspected drug traffickers and seized more than P2 million worth of suspected shabu (crystal methamphetamine) in a buy-bust operation in Batangas City early Friday, police said.

The Calabarzon police said operatives from the Batangas City Drug Enforcement Unit, with assistance from the Philippine Drug Enforcement Agency (PDEA)-Batangas, arrested.

stories.title: ICYMI: Sara Duterte impeachment trial Week 3 highlights
stories.summary:
ICYMI: Sara Duterte impeachment trial Week 3 highlights.

MANILA, Philippines – The impeachment court this week granted the prosecution’s request to subpoena Vice President Sara Duterte’s bank, tax, and Anti-Money Laundering Council records while the House prosecution panel concluded its presentation of evidence on Article IV involving alleged grave threats.

The week also saw the prosecution’s final witness testify that Duterte’s controversial remarks.

stories.title: 3 most wanted among 12 arrested in MIMAROPA manhunt ops
stories.summary:
3 most wanted among 12 arrested in MIMAROPA manhunt ops. CALAPAN City, Oriental Mindoro — Police arrested 12 wanted persons, including three of MIMAROPA (Mindoro, Marinduque, Romblon, Palawan) or Region IV-B’s most wanted fugitives, during simultaneous manhunt operations across the region on July 23.

According to the Police Regional Office-MIMAROPA Friday, among those arrested was the province’s 10th most wanted person in Mansalay, Oriental Mindoro,.

stories.title: Wind signals raised over parts of N. Luzon due to Kiyapo
stories.summary:
Wind signals raised over parts of N. Luzon due to Kiyapo.

MANILA, Philippines — Tropical storm wind signals were hoisted over parts of Northern Luzon on Friday, July 24, as Kiyapo, formerly a tropical depression, further intensified into a tropical storm, the state weather bureau reported.

Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical and Astronomical Services Administration (Pagasa) weather specialist Obet Badrina said during a 5 a.m. weather advisory that. Philippine Daily Inquirer: LIST of cancelled ferry trips due to Kiyapo.

Philippine Daily Inquirer reports that mANILA, Philippines — Ferry operations in parts of Bohol, Masbate, Eastern Leyte and Samar were cancelled on Friday, July 24 as Tropical Storm Kiyapo continued to bring rough sea conditionss, the Philippine Ports Authority (PPA) said.

In an advisory, PPA said the shipping lines with cancelled trips are as follows: Bohol READ: Kiya

stories.summary: Rwanda-Backed Rebels Enter Congo's Safe-Haven City. Residents of Goma reported gunfire and shelling after rebels overran Congolese troops. U.N. officials estimated that more than one million displaced people were now inside the city.

stories.title: Microsoft Surface Laptop 8 review: a quality PC whose trackpad taps you back
stories.summary:
Microsoft Surface Laptop 8 review: a quality PC whose trackpad taps you back.

Snappy performance, long battery life, great keyboard and excellent new haptic touchpad make the best of Windows 11 Microsoft’s Surface laptop for consumers is back, faster and with longer battery life and a hefty price increase because of the high cost of memory and chips.

The Surface Laptop 8 is a straight replacement for the seventh edition from 2024, which was the first of Microsoft’s new generation of ARM-based, Qualcomm-powered PCs designed to better rival Apple’s MacBook Ai.
